titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Gould Fletcher Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 3829877.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday April 24, 2018 at 20:42

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The John Gould Fletcher collection contains material by, about, and relating to American poet John Gould Fletcher.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired through various gifts and purchases, 1942-1964.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The John Gould Fletcher Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Gould Fletcher Collection. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection received preliminary processing at the time of acquisition. Further organization, rehousing, and description was carried out in 2011.

bioghist.head: John Gould Fletcher (1886-1950)
bioghist.p: John Gould Fletcher, born in Little Rock, Arkansas and educated at Phillips Academy and Harvard (1903-1907), was a Pulitzer Prize-winning poet and author. Fletcher lived in England for years before returning home to Arkansas where, in the 1920s and 1930s, he was active with a group of writers and poets known as the Southern Agrarians. Works by Fletcher include Fire and Wine (1913), Goblins and Pagodas (1916), The Black Rock (1928), the autobiography Life is My Song (1937), Selected Poems (1938), for which he was awarded the Pulitzer Prize, The Burning Mountain (1946), and the history Arkansas (1947). Fletcher married twice, in 1916 to Florence Emily "Daisy" Arbuthnot, and in 1936 to childrens' book author Charlie May Simon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The John Gould Fletcher collection contains writings, journals, correspondence, and photographic and other materials by, about, or relating to the poet John Gould Fletcher. The collection features original drafts, many corrected, for individual poems and collections of poems, including Breakers and Granite (1921) and "Rest in the Harbour" (1916-1917), and short essays. In addition to drafts of original writings by Fletcher, the collection contains reproductions of journals and correspondence, and other papers such as documents, clippings, and printed ephemera.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Writings, 1913-1947. II. Journals, undated. III. Correspondence, 1911-1965. IV. Photographic Material, 1951-1960. V. Other Material, 1922-1956.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I. Writings contains original drafts, many corrected, for early writings by John Gould Fletcher. Writings consist chiefly of drafts for individual poems and collections of poems, such as Breakers and Granite (1921), "Rest in the Harbour" (1916-1917), and "The Rock in the Storm," but drafts for a number of short essays can also be found.
Unsorted writings are filed at the end of the alphabetical run of titles.

did.unittitle: "A Carol for the Coincidence of Christmas and Judgement Day"
The Dance Over Fire and Water by Elie Faure

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II. Journals appears to contain typescript transcriptions from notebooks for the dates 1904 through 1949.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III. Correspondence consists chiefly of copies or transcriptions of letters to and from Fletcher and original third-party correspondence, though original letters from Fletcher can also be found.
Dates assigned below represent circa dates for the dates of the original correspondence rather than the copies or transcriptions.
Unsorted correspondence can be found at the end of the alphabetical run of correspondents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV. Photographic Material contains microfilm and slide transparencies of material relating to John Gould Fletcher which appears to have been assembled by Yale professor Norman Holmes Pearson for purposes of research on Fletcher and/or curation of the library's Fletcher collection. Microfilm documents correspondence between Fletcher and others and slide transparencies document locations in Arkansas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series V. Other Material consists of material by, about, or relating to John Gould Fletcher which appears to have been assembled by Yale professor Norman Holmes Pearson for purposes of research on Fletcher and/or curation of the library's Fletcher collection. Materials include original documents, copies of writings, correspondence, clippings, and printed ephemera.
